Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).

let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in

- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6

  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)
  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)

OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in

- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).

- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).

- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)
  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)
  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)
  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)
  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

Puzzle description:
HM-TRACE: Hindley-Milner type inference puzzle.

You must execute deterministic Algorithm W on a program and report:
- Principal type schemes for specific let-bindings
- The final result type
- The binding trace length M and specific bindings at checkpoint positions


OBJECT LANGUAGE:

Syntax (terms):
  e ::= x                        (variable)
      | (\x. e)                  (lambda abstraction)
      | (e e)                    (application)
      | let x = e in e           (let binding)
      | (e, e)                   (pair)
      | fst e                    (first projection)
      | snd e                    (second projection)
      | true | false             (boolean literals)
      | 0                        (natural zero)
      | succ e                   (successor)
      | isZero e                 (zero test)
      | if e then e else e       (conditional)

Syntax (types):
  τ ::= Bool                     (boolean type)
      | Nat                      (natural number type)
      | α_k                      (type variable with index k)
      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)
  j=1: i=2; Var 1
  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

      | (τ -> τ)                 (function type)
      | (τ × τ)                  (product type)

Type schemes:
  σ ::= ∀ α_{{i1}} ... α_{{it}}. τ
Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

Primitives in the initial environment Γ0:
  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
  snd   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> b
  succ  : Nat -> Nat
  isZero: Nat -> Bool
  if    : ∀a. Bool -> a -> a -> a

IMPORTANT NOTE (about variable indices):
  Quantified variables in schemes (like a,b above) are placeholders and are NOT allocated from the global fresh-variable counter next_tv.
  Instantiation (event I) always allocates fresh α_k from next_tv and substitutes these placeholders simultaneously.

DETERMINISTIC ALGORITHM W (Damas-Milner) AND TRACE DEFINITION:

2.1 Fresh type-variable allocation (global counter):
Maintain a global counter next_tv starting at 0. A "fresh type var" is α_{{next_tv}} and increments next_tv.

Freshness events (and ONLY these events) allocate fresh type variables:
  (L) On entering a lambda \x. e, allocate a fresh α_k for the binder x before inferring the body.
  (A) On typing an application (e1 e2), allocate a fresh α_k as the result type before calling unification.
  (I) On instantiating a polymorphic scheme ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ, allocate fresh variables for each quantified variable in the order i1,...,it and substitute simultaneously.

No other fresh variables are permitted. next_tv is shared globally across the whole program.

2.2 Algorithm W (standard, fixed traversal order):

Type inference traverses syntax left-to-right.

W(Γ, x):
  look up σ = Γ(x); return (id, instantiate(σ)).

W(Γ, \x. e):
  allocate α_k via (L) for x
  compute (S, τ) = W(Γ[x ↦ α_k], e)
  return (S, S(α_k) -> τ)

W(Γ, e1 e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(S1Γ, e2)
  allocate α_k via (A) as fresh r
  S3 = unify(S2(τ1), (τ2 -> r))
  return (S3∘S2∘S1, S3(r))

W(Γ, (e1,e2)):
  infer e1 then e2 left-to-right, composing substitutions as standard
  return product type of the inferred subtypes

W(Γ, fst e) and snd e:
  treat fst/snd as variables in Γ0, i.e. inference proceeds via lookup+instantiate+application; do not special-case.

W(Γ, succ e), isZero e, and if c then e1 else e2:
  treat succ/isZero/if as variables in Γ0 as well.
  You may desugar:
    succ e            ≡ (succ e)
    isZero e          ≡ (isZero e)
    if c then e1 else e2  ≡ (((if c) e1) e2)
  and then apply only the Var/App/Let/Lam/Pair rules above.

W(Γ, let x = e1 in e2):
  (S1, τ1) = W(Γ, e1)
  Γ1 = S1Γ
  σ  = generalise(Γ1, τ1)
  (S2, τ2) = W(Γ1[x ↦ σ], e2)
  return (S2∘S1, τ2)

Generalise(Γ, τ):
  quantify exactly the type variables free in τ but not free in Γ.
  Quantifier order is increasing numeric id of α_k.
  return ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ

Instantiate(∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ):
  allocate fresh β1..βt via (I), and substitute α_{{ij}} ↦ βj simultaneously.
  i1..it are exactly as stored in the scheme (which is increasing id under Generalise).

2.3 Unification algorithm (FIFO queue) and the recorded binding trace:

Use Robinson unification with a FIFO equation queue and occurs check. All unify calls made during W contribute to ONE global trace in temporal order.

unify(t, u):
  S = id
  Q = FIFO queue initialised with (t, u)
  while Q not empty:
    pop front (t1,u1)
    t1 := S(t1); u1 := S(u1)
    if t1 == u1: continue
    elif t1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in u1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := u1
        RECORD this binding (α_i := u1) as the next element of the global binding trace
    elif u1 is Var α_i:
      if α_i occurs in t1: FAIL
      else:
        extend S with binding α_i := t1
        RECORD it
    elif t1 = Arr(a1,b1) and u1 = Arr(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    elif t1 = Prod(a1,b1) and u1 = Prod(a2,b2):
      enqueue (a1,a2) then (b1,b2)
    else FAIL

Binding trace B:
  Over the entire W run on the whole program, concatenate the recorded bindings in the exact order they are recorded.
  Let M = |B| be the total number of recorded bindings (this is the "unification step count").

2.4 Prefix format for recorded bindings (certificate output format):

A binding is printed as:
  i=<decimal i>; <prefix(τ)>

Prefix type format is:
  Bool
  Nat
  Var <decimal k>
  Arr (<T1>) (<T2>)
  Prod (<T1>) (<T2>)

Parentheses are mandatory around Arr/Prod children and forbidden elsewhere except those.
Examples:
  i=7; Nat
  i=12; Arr (Var 3) (Prod (Nat) (Var 9))
  i=5; Prod (Arr (Var 1) (Var 2)) (Bool)

TYPE SCHEME NORMALIZATION (for Q1-Q3):

When reporting principal type schemes, use a normalised human format independent of internal α_k ids:

Scheme printing:
  If no quantifiers: print τ
  Else: print "forall a0 a1 ... . τ" with the listed a_j separated by single spaces.

Type printing (human format):
  Bool, Nat, a_j (for type variables)
  (t1 × t2) (for products, always parenthesized)
  t1 -> t2 (for arrows, right associative; parenthesise left argument if it is an arrow)

Normalisation of quantified vars:
  Take the scheme σ = ∀α_{{i1}}...α_{{it}}. τ from the verifier.
  Replace these quantified vars by placeholders a0..a(t-1) in order of FIRST APPEARANCE when printing τ left-to-right.
  (So even if σ quantifies α_7,α_2, the renaming is by appearance in printed τ, not by id.)

The type of result (Q3) must be monomorphic (no forall).


Example:

Program (N = 3):
let t0 = (\x. x) in
let t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)) in
let t2 = (t1 0) in
t2

Execution trace (abbreviated):
- t0 = (\x. x): allocates α_0 for x, infers body type α_0, generalises to ∀α_0. α_0 -> α_0
- t1 = (\y. (t0 y, t0 true)):
  - allocates α_1 for y
  - instantiates t0 twice with fresh vars (α_2, α_3)
  - unifies and records bindings
  - generalises to ∀α_1. α_1 -> (α_1 × Bool)
- t2 = (t1 0): instantiates t1 with α_4, applies to 0, unifies
- result: (Nat × Bool)

Q1: Principal type scheme of t0: forall a0 . a0 -> a0
Q2: Principal type scheme of t1: forall a0 . a0 -> (a0 × Bool)
Q3: Type of result: (Nat × Bool)
Q4: M = 6
Q5: (example bindings)

  j=3: i=4; Bool
  j=6: i=7; Prod (Nat) (Bool)

Quantifiers bind distinct type variables; order matters for determinism.

  fst   : ∀a b. (a × b) -> a
